100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 19, 1926, the British Empire faced growing tensions as the General Strike loomed, with workers demanding better wages and working conditions. In the United States, the Senate debated the controversial tariff bill, reflecting ongoing economic concerns. Meanwhile, in France, the government struggled to stabilize following the resignation of Prime Minister Édouard Herriot, leading to uncertainty in the wake of political upheaval.
